## PARTIES

This Statement of Work ("SOW" or "Agreement") is entered into as of **[EFFECTIVE_DATE]** ("Effective Date")

**BETWEEN:**

**1. [PROVIDER_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, 2013, bearing CIN **[PROVIDER_CIN]**, GSTIN **[PROVIDER_GSTIN]**, PAN **[PROVIDER_PAN]**, having its registered office at **[PROVIDER_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Service Provider" or "Provider");

**AND**

**2. [CLIENT_LEGAL_NAME]**, a company incorporated under the Companies Act, [CLIENT_INCORPORATION_YEAR], bearing CIN **[CLIENT_CIN]**, GSTIN **[CLIENT_GSTIN]**, PAN **[CLIENT_PAN]**, having its registered office at **[CLIENT_ADDRESS]**, India (hereinafter "Client").

> **Note:** Where this SOW is executed under a Master Services Agreement ("MSA"), the terms of the MSA apply to this SOW and govern in the event of conflict, unless this SOW expressly states otherwise. Where no MSA exists, this SOW is a standalone agreement and incorporates all provisions required under Indian law.

---

## RECITALS

(A) Provider has the expertise and capability to provide the services described in this SOW.

(B) Client wishes to engage Provider to perform the project described herein on a fixed-price basis, and Provider is willing to do so on the terms set out in this SOW.

(C) Both Parties have agreed on the project scope, deliverables, milestones, and commercial terms as set out below.

**NOW THEREFORE**, the Parties agree as follows:

---

## 1. DEFINITIONS

**1.1 "Acceptance"** means Client's written approval of a Deliverable confirming that it meets the Acceptance Criteria.

**1.2 "Acceptance Criteria"** means the functional, technical, and quality standards against which each Deliverable is tested and evaluated, as set out in Schedule 2.

**1.3 "Acceptance Period"** means the period within which Client must review and either accept or reject a Deliverable, as specified in Clause 5.

**1.4 "Change Request"** means a written request by either Party to modify the Scope, Deliverables, Timeline, or Fixed Price under this SOW.

**1.5 "Deliverable"** means each work product, software module, document, or output listed in Schedule 1 that Provider must complete and deliver to Client.

**1.6 "Fixed Price"** means the total fixed fee payable by Client to Provider for all Deliverables under this SOW, as set out in Clause 7.

**1.7 "Milestone"** means a defined project stage at which one or more Deliverables are due and a Milestone Payment becomes payable, as set out in Schedule 3.

**1.8 "Milestone Payment"** means the portion of the Fixed Price payable on achievement and Acceptance of each Milestone.

**1.9 "Project"** means the project described in Clause 2 of this SOW.

**1.10 "Project Manager"** means the individual designated by each Party as the primary point of contact for the Project.

**1.11 "Scope"** means the work to be performed under this SOW as described in Schedule 1.

**1.12 "Timeline"** means the project schedule including start date, Milestones, and target completion date, as set out in Schedule 3.

## 3. SCOPE OF WORK

The detailed scope of work, including all Deliverables, functional requirements, technical specifications, and exclusions, is set out in **Schedule 1** to this SOW.

**3.1 In-Scope:** The work expressly described in Schedule 1.

**3.2 Out-of-Scope:** Any work not expressly described in Schedule 1 is out of scope and requires a Change Request under Clause 9 to be added to the Project.

**3.3 Assumptions:** The delivery of the Scope is based on the assumptions listed in Schedule 1. If any assumption proves incorrect and materially impacts the Scope or Timeline, Provider shall notify Client within five (5) business days and the Parties shall agree on the impact via the change management process.

---

## 4. PROJECT GOVERNANCE

**4.1 Project Managers.**

| Role | Provider | Client |
|---|---|---|
| **Project Manager** | [PROVIDER_PM_NAME] | [CLIENT_PM_NAME] |
| **Email** | [PROVIDER_PM_EMAIL] | [CLIENT_PM_EMAIL] |
| **Phone** | [PROVIDER_PM_PHONE] | [CLIENT_PM_PHONE] |

**4.2 Progress Reporting.** Provider shall provide Client with written progress reports: (a) weekly status updates covering completed work, pending items, risks, and next steps; and (b) Milestone completion reports at the conclusion of each Milestone.

**4.3 Steering Committee.** The Parties may establish a joint steering committee to review project progress, resolve escalations, and approve significant decisions. Composition and meeting frequency to be agreed in writing.

**4.4 Client Responsibilities.** Client shall: (a) provide timely access to systems, data, and personnel required by Provider; (b) review and respond to Deliverables within the Acceptance Period; (c) make decisions within three (3) business days of request; and (d) assign a suitably empowered Project Manager.

---

## 5. DELIVERY AND ACCEPTANCE

**5.1 Delivery.** Provider shall deliver each Deliverable on or before the date specified in Schedule 3. Delivery shall be by [DELIVERY_METHOD — e.g., electronic submission to Client's designated repository / email / client portal].

**5.2 Acceptance Period.** Upon delivery, Client shall review each Deliverable within **ten (10) business days** ("Acceptance Period") and shall provide Provider with either: (a) written Acceptance confirming the Deliverable meets the Acceptance Criteria; or (b) written rejection specifying in reasonable detail the defects or non-conformances identified.

**5.3 Deemed Acceptance.** If Client does not provide written Acceptance or a written rejection notice within the Acceptance Period, the Deliverable shall be deemed accepted.

**5.4 Correction and Resubmission.** If a Deliverable is rejected, Provider shall correct the identified defects at no additional cost and resubmit within **five (5) business days** (or such other period as agreed). Client shall review the corrected Deliverable within **five (5) business days** of resubmission.

**5.5 Persistent Rejection.** If a Deliverable is rejected twice on the same or substantially similar grounds and Provider believes the Acceptance Criteria are met, either Party may escalate the matter to senior representatives and, if unresolved, to the dispute resolution mechanism in Clause 16.

---

## 6. KEY PERSONNEL

**6.1** The following individuals are designated as Key Personnel for this Project:

| Name | Role | Provider / Subcontractor |
|---|---|---|
| [KEY_PERSON_1_NAME] | [ROLE] | Provider |
| [KEY_PERSON_2_NAME] | [ROLE] | Provider |

**6.2** Provider shall not replace, reassign, or remove Key Personnel without: (a) not less than **twenty-one (21) calendar days'** prior written notice to Client; (b) Client's prior written consent, not to be unreasonably withheld; and (c) a structured handover and knowledge transfer of at least ten (10) business days before the change takes effect.

**6.3** If Key Personnel become unavailable due to unforeseen circumstances, Provider shall notify Client within two (2) business days and propose a replacement of equivalent or superior skill and experience within five (5) business days.

---

## 7. FIXED PRICE AND PAYMENT

**7.1 Fixed Price.** The total Fixed Price for all Deliverables under this SOW is:

**₹[FIXED_PRICE_IN_INR] (Indian Rupees [FIXED_PRICE_IN_WORDS] only)**

exclusive of applicable GST and taxes.

**7.2 Milestone Payment Schedule.**

| Milestone | Deliverable(s) | Due Date | Payment (₹) | % of Fixed Price |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Milestone 1 — [NAME] | [DELIVERABLE_LIST] | [DATE] | ₹[AMOUNT] | [%]% |
| Milestone 2 — [NAME] | [DELIVERABLE_LIST] | [DATE] | ₹[AMOUNT] | [%]% |
| Milestone 3 — [NAME] | [DELIVERABLE_LIST] | [DATE] | ₹[AMOUNT] | [%]% |
| **Final Milestone** | All remaining Deliverables | [DATE] | ₹[AMOUNT] | [%]% |
| **TOTAL** | | | **₹[TOTAL]** | **100%** |

**7.3 Invoicing.** Provider shall invoice Client upon written Acceptance of each Milestone (or upon deemed acceptance under Clause 5.3). Invoices shall comply with GST law requirements including HSN/SAC code, GSTIN, and applicable tax breakup.

**7.4 Payment Terms.** Client shall pay each Milestone Payment within **thirty (30) calendar days** of the invoice date.

**7.5 Late Payment.** Overdue amounts accrue interest at **1.5% per month** from the due date until actual payment.

---

## 8. TAXES AND GST (M-4)

**8.1 Exclusive of Tax.** All amounts are exclusive of GST and all other applicable taxes, levies, duties, and cesses.

**8.2 GST.** GST at the applicable rate shall be charged additionally per the CGST Act, 2017, IGST Act, 2017, and applicable State GST Act.

**8.3 TDS.** Where Client is required to deduct tax at source under the Income Tax Act, 1961, Client shall: (a) deduct TDS at the applicable rate at the time of credit or payment, whichever is earlier; (b) deposit the deducted amount with the tax authority within the prescribed time; (c) issue a valid TDS certificate (Form 16A or applicable) within the prescribed period; and (d) file required TDS returns within prescribed due dates.

**8.4 Input Tax Credit.** Each party is responsible for its own GST compliance and shall ensure timely filing of returns to facilitate input tax credit for the other party.

**8.5 Currency.** All amounts are in Indian Rupees (INR). Payment by electronic transfer (NEFT/RTGS/IMPS) to Provider's designated bank account.

---

## 9. CHANGE MANAGEMENT

**9.1** Either Party may request a change to the Scope, Deliverables, Timeline, or Fixed Price by submitting a written Change Request to the other Party's Project Manager.

**9.2** Within **five (5) business days** of receipt of a Change Request, Provider shall provide Client with a written impact assessment specifying: (a) the proposed changes to Scope and Deliverables; (b) the impact on the Timeline and Milestones; (c) any adjustment to the Fixed Price; and (d) any other material impacts.

**9.3** A Change Request becomes binding only when executed in writing by authorised representatives of both Parties ("Change Order"). No change to the SOW is effective unless made pursuant to a signed Change Order.

**9.4** Provider shall not commence work on any change until a signed Change Order is in place. If Client requests Provider to commence work before a Change Order is signed, Provider may (but is not obligated to) do so, and the Parties shall execute the Change Order promptly thereafter.

---

## 10. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY

**10.1 Deliverables.** Unless otherwise specified in Schedule 1, all Intellectual Property Rights in Deliverables created specifically for Client under this SOW shall vest exclusively in Client upon full payment of the Fixed Price.

**10.2 Background IP.** Provider retains all rights in its pre-existing tools, frameworks, methodologies, and libraries ("Background IP"). Provider grants Client a non-exclusive, perpetual, royalty-free licence to use Background IP embedded in Deliverables for the purposes for which the Deliverables were created.

**10.3 Open Source.** Where Provider uses open-source software in Deliverables, Provider shall: (a) disclose all open-source components and their applicable licences; (b) ensure no open-source licence imposes obligations on Client beyond what is permissible; and (c) seek prior written approval from Client before using any copyleft-licensed components.

---

## 11. CONFIDENTIALITY

Both Parties shall hold the other's Confidential Information in strict confidence, use it solely for the purposes of this SOW, and not disclose it to any third party without prior written consent. Confidentiality obligations survive termination of this SOW for three (3) years from the date of disclosure.

---

## 12. WARRANTIES

**12.1** Provider warrants that: (a) it has full authority to enter into this SOW; (b) the Services will be performed with reasonable skill and care; (c) Deliverables will conform to the Acceptance Criteria; and (d) Provider will not knowingly incorporate any third-party materials in Deliverables that infringe any third-party intellectual property rights.

**12.2 Defect Warranty.** For a period of **ninety (90) days** after Acceptance of each Deliverable ("Warranty Period"), Provider shall, at no additional cost, promptly correct any material defects or non-conformances identified by Client. This warranty does not apply to defects caused by Client's modification of Deliverables or use outside the intended purpose.

---

## 13. TERM AND TERMINATION

**13.1 Term.** This SOW commences on the Effective Date and continues until all Deliverables have been accepted and all payments have been made, or until earlier termination under this clause.

**13.2 Termination for Cause.** Either Party may terminate this SOW immediately on written notice if the other Party commits a material breach and fails to cure it within thirty (30) days of written notice.

**13.3 Termination for Convenience.** Client may terminate this SOW for convenience on thirty (30) days' written notice. On such termination, Client shall pay Provider for all work satisfactorily completed up to the termination date, plus any unavoidable committed costs. Provider shall cease work and mitigate costs on receipt of notice.

**13.4 Effects of Termination.** Provider shall deliver all completed and partially completed Deliverables to Client; Client shall pay for work satisfactorily completed. Clauses 10, 11, 14, 15, 16, and 17 survive termination.

---

## 14. FORCE MAJEURE (M-5)

**14.1** Neither party shall be liable for delay or failure to perform (other than payment obligations) caused by a Force Majeure Event: acts of God, natural disasters, fire, government-declared pandemic, war, terrorism, civil unrest, acts of government, nationwide strikes, third-party telecom or power failure, or court orders.

**14.2 Exclusions.** Economic downturns, loss of revenue, inability to pay money, foreseeable technical failures, or staffing shortages from management decisions are NOT Force Majeure Events.

**14.3 Notice and Mitigation.** Affected Party notifies in writing within five (5) business days, uses commercially reasonable efforts to mitigate, and provides regular updates.

**14.4 Extended Events.** If the event continues for sixty (60) consecutive days, either party may terminate on thirty (30) days' written notice without liability, except for amounts due for work already performed.

---

## 15. LIMITATION OF LIABILITY (M-8)

**(a)** EXCEPT AS IN CLAUSE 15(c), NEITHER PARTY SHALL BE LIABLE FOR INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, OR FOR LOSS OF PROFITS, REVENUE, GOODWILL, DATA, OR BUSINESS OPPORTUNITY, HOWEVER ARISING.

**(b)** EXCEPT AS IN CLAUSE 15(c), TOTAL AGGREGATE LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED: (i) WHERE THE SOW HAS BEEN IN FORCE FOR TWELVE (12) OR MORE MONTHS — FEES PAID IN THE TWELVE (12) MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM; OR (ii) WHERE THE SOW HAS BEEN IN FORCE FOR LESS THAN TWELVE (12) MONTHS — TOTAL FEES PAID FROM THE EFFECTIVE DATE TO THE DATE OF THE CLAIM.

**(c)** Clauses 15(a) and 15(b) do NOT apply to: (i) death or personal injury from negligence; (ii) fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; (iii) IP indemnification obligations; (iv) DPDP Act obligations including Personal Data Breach liability; (v) wilful misconduct or gross negligence; or (vi) liability that cannot be excluded under applicable Indian law.

**(d)** Each party shall take all reasonable steps to mitigate any loss and notify the other party promptly of any circumstance likely to give rise to a claim.

---

## 16. ELECTRONIC EXECUTION AND DIGITAL SIGNATURES (M-2)

Electronic signatures are valid, binding, and enforceable under the Information Technology Act, 2000 (as amended 2008) and the IT (Certifying Authorities) Rules, 2000. A digitally signed copy transmitted electronically is treated as an original. Neither party shall contest validity solely on grounds of electronic execution. This SOW may be executed in counterparts, all constituting one instrument.

---

## 17. GOVERNING LAW AND DISPUTE RESOLUTION (M-1)

**(a) Negotiation.** Disputes first referred to senior representatives for good faith resolution within thirty (30) days of written notice.

**(b) Arbitration.** Unresolved disputes finally resolved by binding arbitration under the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996 (as amended 2021). Sole arbitrator mutually appointed within fifteen (15) days, failing which appointed by the High Court of Karnataka. Seat and venue: Bengaluru, Karnataka, India. Language: English. Award: final and binding.

**(c) Interim Relief.** Either party may seek interim or injunctive relief from courts in Bengaluru, Karnataka, India.

**(d) Exclusive Jurisdiction.** Courts at Bengaluru, Karnataka, India for non-arbitration matters.

**(e) Costs.** Each party bears its own costs unless otherwise ordered.
